items.title: Backpropagation through layers
items.description: Each neural network layer implements a map whose Jacobian (or vector-Jacobian product) propagates output gradients back to inputs and parameters via the chain rule.

items.title: Newton and quasi-Newton optimization
items.description: Second-order methods use the Hessian (or an approximation like L-BFGS) to precondition gradient steps, adapting step size per curvature direction on the loss surface.

items.title: Loss landscape analysis
items.description: Hessian eigenvalues near trained weights reveal sharp vs flat minima; extreme curvature correlates with sensitivity to perturbations and optimizer behavior.

items.title: Change of variables in generative models
items.description: Normalizing flows and density estimation require the log-determinant of the Jacobian of an invertible transformation to track volume change under the map.
